name: Anti-Gravity Craft
description: The concept of Anti-Gravity Craft in America primarily revolves around the belief that the US government, often in collaboration with clandestine organizations, has secretly developed and possesses operational aircraft utilizing technology that counteracts or negates gravity. This theory often links to recovered extraterrestrial vehicles (UFOs) and reverse engineering efforts. Proponents suggest that this technology has been suppressed to maintain dependence on fossil fuels and control global power structures. Key evidence cited includes alleged leaked documents (often of dubious authenticity), eyewitness accounts of unusual aerial phenomena, and claims of whistleblowers who purportedly worked on such projects. The mainstream scientific perspective dismisses these claims, citing a lack of verifiable evidence, the violation of established physics principles, and the implausibility of maintaining such a large-scale secret for so long. Explanations for reported sightings typically involve misidentification of conventional aircraft, natural phenomena, or hoaxes. Furthermore, the immense technological and logistical hurdles of achieving true anti-gravity are deemed insurmountable with currently understood science. The theory gained traction in the mid-20th century, fueled by Cold War paranoia and growing public interest in UFOs, and continues to persist in various forms online and in popular culture.
subtitle: Secret technology defying gravity's pull.

claims.claim: The US government possesses working anti-gravity aircraft.
claims.evidence: Alleged leaked documents (e.g., Majestic 12), whistleblower testimonies (e.g., Bob Lazar).
claims.criticism: Lack of verifiable documentation, inconsistencies in whistleblower accounts, no independent confirmation.

claims.claim: Anti-gravity technology was reverse-engineered from crashed UFOs.
claims.evidence: Roswell incident claims, supposed recovered alien technology.
claims.criticism: Roswell explanations involving weather balloons and military projects, lack of physical evidence of alien technology.

claims.claim: The existence of anti-gravity craft is suppressed to protect the oil industry.
claims.evidence: General arguments about powerful vested interests controlling technological development.
claims.criticism: Complex technological and economic barriers to widespread adoption even if the technology existed; oil industry is a complex system, not a single entity with unified control.

keyFigures.name: Bob Lazar
keyFigures.role: Claimed to have worked on reverse-engineering alien technology at Area 51.

keyFigures.name: Stanton Friedman
keyFigures.role: Ufologist who investigated and promoted the idea of government cover-ups.

keyFigures.name: Donald Menzel
keyFigures.role: Astronomer and UFO skeptic who debunked many anti-gravity claims.

mythologyConnections: Flying chariots of the gods (ancient interpretations)
Legends of levitation and superhuman flight

timeline.event: Roswell incident sparks UFO and conspiracy theories.
Cold War paranoia fuels speculation about secret government projects.
Bob Lazar's claims about Area 51 and anti-gravity technology gain widespread attention.
Continued online discussions and media portrayals perpetuate the anti-gravity craft theory.

sources.author: Annie Jacobsen
sources.work: Area 51: An Uncensored History of America's Top Secret Military Base
sources.perspective: neutral (presents various perspectives, including conspiracy theories)
sources.title: Annie Jacobsen

sources.author: Donald Menzel

sources.title: Donald Menzel

sources.author: Richard Dolan

sources.title: Richard Dolan

variants: USO (Unidentified Submerged Objects - often linked to similar tech)
